content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/datasources/dataimportcron/actions/DataImportCronActions.scss

Apply ALL of the following 4 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 7
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-dropdown' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-dropdown

Code context:
```
    6  
    7    .pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu {
    8      min-width: 200px;

```

### Fix 2
Line: 7
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown-menu]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-dropdown__menu' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu

Code context:
```
    6  
    7    .pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu {
    8      min-width: 200px;

```

### Fix 3
Line: 7
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-dropdown' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-dropdown

Code context:
```
    6  
    7    .pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu {
    8      min-width: 200px;

```

### Fix 4
Line: 7
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown-menu]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-dropdown__menu' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu

Code context:
```
    6  
    7    .pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu {
    8      min-width: 200px;

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/datasources/dataimportcron/actions/DataImportCronActions.scss
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its contents.

Now let me reason through each fix:

**Fix 1 & Fix 3**: Both target the `pf-v5-c-dropdown` class reference on line 7. The class `.pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu` contains the block `pf-v5-c-dropdown` as a BEM prefix. This class has been removed with no v6 equivalent.

**Fix 2 & Fix 4**: Both target the `pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu` class reference on line 7. This is the full BEM selector. It has been removed with no v6 equivalent.

All four fixes target the same line (line 7) and the same CSS rule block (lines 7-9). The class `pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu` was removed entirely — there's no `pf-v6-c-dropdown__menu` replacement. The fix is to remove this dead CSS rule.

Looking at what remains: the `.kubevirt-data-source-actions` block would only contain the `ul` rule. That's still valid and should be preserved.

Let me check if this selector is referenced anywhere else:
The only reference is in this SCSS file itself. The removed CSS class is only used in this one rule. I'll remove the dead CSS rule block (lines 7-9) and clean up the resulting empty line.
Let me verify the final file:

The file looks correct. The dead CSS rule targeting the removed `pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu` class has been removed, and the remaining `ul` styling rule within `.kubevirt-data-source-actions` is preserved.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Detail |
|-----|------|--------|--------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown` | ✅ Applied | Removed the `.pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu` rule block (lines 7-9) which contained the dead `pf-v5-c-dropdown` BEM block reference. No v6 equivalent exists. |
| Fix 2 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown-menu` | ✅ Applied | Same removal — the `.pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu` selector on line 7 was the full class targeted by this fix. |
| Fix 3 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown` | ✅ Applied | Same removal — the `pf-v5-c-dropdown` block class is part of the removed selector. |
| Fix 4 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown-menu` | ✅ Applied | Same removal — the `pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu` element class is the removed selector. |

All four fixes targeted the same CSS rule block (`.pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu { min-width: 200px; }`) on line 7. The entire rule was removed since both the BEM block (`pf-v5-c-dropdown`) and element (`pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu`) classes were removed in PF v6 with no equivalent replacements. The `min-width: 200px` override is no longer meaningful without the target class. The remaining `.kubevirt-data-source-actions ul` styling is preserved unchanged.
